The result returned by the agent is not visible to the user. To show the user the result, you should send a text message back to the user with a concise summary of the result.\n- Each agent invocation is stateless. You will not be able to send additional messages to the agent, nor will the agent be able to communicate with you outside of its final report. data: {"content_index":0,"item_id":"","logprobs":[],"output_index":0,"sequence_number":6305,"text":"\u003canalysis\u003e\n[Chronological Review: The conversation began with UI redesign of a VS Code extension dashboard. Initial inspection mistakenly looked at `frontend/src/App.tsx`, but the actual target UI was located in `vscode-extension/src/dashboard.ts` (and older dev mirror `dashboard.dev.ts`). The dashboard was redesigned multiple times: user identity info moved into the header, the separate user card removed, “新会话” first minimized then removed, offline state made more visible, the flame icon removed, layout made calmer and more compact, and later the identity section was made more horizontal to reduce blank space.  \nNext, the user asked to “处理问题并更新扩展”. Investigation found TypeScript compile errors due to missing runtime/test type declarations. `vscode-extension/tsconfig.json` was updated to include `node`, `vscode`, and `jest`, restoring compilation. Extension versions were bumped from `0.1.0` to `0.1.1` and `0.1.2`, then rebuilt/installed locally.  \nThe user then shifted to packaging and asked to check scripts for “生成各个系统扩展包的脚本”. Analysis showed packaging claimed multiple targets but only had a Windows client binary. Work then expanded to real cross-platform build support: extension packaging scripts were fixed to validate target-specific binaries; new Go stubs and build scripts were added for macOS/Linux; client cross-builds and VSIX packaging for win/mac/mac-arm/linux were validated; extension packaging was made able to auto-build missing client binaries.  \nAfter packaging work, the user reported runtime/data issues: new users saw token consumption for employee `10001`, and refresh didn’t seem to work. Investigation showed dashboard cards were reading the global `/api/dashboard/overview` summary instead of the current employee’s `/api/clients/my-stats`, and local tracker state was stored in global keys that could leak across changed user IDs. `vscode-extension/src/dashboard.ts` was changed so refresh now flushes pending queue and syncs current employee stats; `vscode-extension/src/tokenTracker.ts` was changed to scope `todayStats` and `offlineQueue` by `serverUrl + userId`. Tests and packaging were rerun, and extension version advanced through `0.1.3`, `0.1.4`, and `0.1.5`.  \nThe user then asked about new users being recorded on the server. Investigation showed extension-side data only truly created `users` rows when `/api/collect` or `/api/clients/my-stats` path persisted data; heartbeat only wrote `clients`. `backend/app/routers/collect.py` was updated so heartbeat also persists user identity, `my-stats` commits after creating/updating the user, and incomplete identity heartbeats are ignored.  \nThen the user asked about duplicate employee IDs and similar details, while keeping core logic stable and adding friendly control-panel hints. The backend was enhanced with conflict detection logic: same employee ID + different name is treated as a conflict and now prevented from overwriting existing user data; same employee ID + same name is allowed, including across multiple apps. A lightweight `/api/clients/identity-check` endpoint was added to return statuses like `incomplete`, `new`, `matched`, `warning`, and `conflict`, plus existing name, other employee IDs with same name, and known apps. On the extension side, `vscode-extension/src/dashboard.ts` was updated to call this identity-check endpoint on refresh/save and to show a friendly identity status panel in the basic settings area, with green/yellow/red styling, input highlighting, and explicit wording that one employee can legitimately use multiple apps. Extension version was bumped to `0.1.6`, packaged, and installed locally.  \nFinally, the user asked: “帮我部署一下服务端”. The agent started working on server deployment, aiming to deploy the backend changes without disrupting more than necessary.]\n\n[Intent Mapping: \n1. Investigation showed dashboard cards were reading the global `/api/dashboard/overview` summary instead of the current employee’s `/api/clients/my-stats`, and local tracker state was stored in global keys that could leak across changed user IDs. `vscode-extension/src/dashboard.ts` was changed so refresh now flushes pending queue and syncs current employee stats; `vscode-extension/src/tokenTracker.ts` was changed to scope `todayStats` and `offlineQueue` by `serverUrl + userId`. Investigation showed extension-side data only truly created `users` rows when `/api/collect` or `/api/clients/my-stats` path persisted data; heartbeat only wrote `clients`. `backend/app/routers/collect.py` was updated so heartbeat also persists user identity, `my-stats` commits after creating/updating the user, and incomplete identity heartbeats are ignored.  \nThen the user asked about duplicate employee IDs and similar details, while keeping core logic stable and adding friendly control-panel hints. The backend was enhanced with conflict detection logic: same employee ID + different name is treated as a conflict and now prevented from overwriting existing user data; same employee ID + same name is allowed, including across multiple apps. A lightweight `/api/clients/identity-check` endpoint was added to return statuses like `incomplete`, `new`, `matched`, `warning`, and `conflict`, plus existing name, other employee IDs with same name, and known apps. On the extension side, `vscode-extension/src/dashboard.ts` was updated to call this identity-check endpoint on refresh/save and to show a friendly identity status panel in the basic settings area, with green/yellow/red styling, input highlighting, and explicit wording that one employee can legitimately use multiple apps. Extension version was bumped to `0.1.6`, packaged, and installed locally.  \nFinally, the user asked: “帮我部署一下服务端”. Investigation showed dashboard cards were reading the global `/api/dashboard/overview` summary instead of the current employee’s `/api/clients/my-stats`, and local tracker state was stored in global keys that could leak across changed user IDs. `vscode-extension/src/dashboard.ts` was changed so refresh now flushes pending queue and syncs current employee stats; `vscode-extension/src/tokenTracker.ts` was changed to scope `todayStats` and `offlineQueue` by `serverUrl + userId`. The backend was enhanced with conflict detection logic: same employee ID + different name is treated as a conflict and now prevented from overwriting existing user data; same employee ID + same name is allowed, including across multiple apps. A lightweight `/api/clients/identity-check` endpoint was added to return statuses like `incomplete`, `new`, `matched`, `warning`, and `conflict`, plus existing name, other employee IDs with same name, and known apps. On the extension side, `vscode-extension/src/dashboard.ts` was updated to call this identity-check endpoint on refresh/save and to show a friendly identity status panel in the basic settings area, with green/yellow/red styling, input highlighting, and explicit wording that one employee can legitimately use multiple apps.
